appraisal_events.evidence: Elle « trouvât Bergotte plus spirituel que M. de marquis de Bréauté »; le narrateur ajoute que ces jugements « subversifs… [sont] malgré tout, justes » et portés par de « rares personnes supérieures ».
appraisal_events.explanation: The duchess values Bergotte above the Marquis de Bréauté; the narrator frames this preference as just and a sign of a superiority of discernment.

status_effects.explanation: He is locally elevated as wittier than Bréauté by a recognized arbiter.
Her “subversive” judgment is presented as just and characteristic of the rare superior persons, which reinforces her authority of taste.
